data.question: Is the QRS duration prolonged on this ECG? Note that a QRS duration is considered to be prolonged if it is greater than 120 milliseconds.
In which of the following segments can you observe a QRS wave that demonstrates the prolonged QRS duration? The options below refer to time ranges on the ECG strip, provided in seconds.
For the selected segment, which range does the measured QRS duration fall into? The ranges are inclusive of the lower bound but exclusive of the upper bound.
Does this ECG show a dominant S wave in leads V1 and V2, where the amplitude of the negative deflection in the QRS complex is greater than the amplitude of the positive deflection?
Within the leads V1 and V2, in which of the following segments can you observe the QRS complex with dominant S wave? The options below refer to time ranges on the ECG strip, provided in seconds.
Does this ECG show a monophasic positive QRS wave in at least two of leads I, aVL, and V6?
Which of the following leads show the monophasic positive QRS wave? Select all possible leads from the options below.
Within the selected leads, in which of the following segments can you observe the monophasic positive QRS wave? The options below refer to time ranges on the ECG strip, provided in seconds.
Does this ECG show a notched QRS complex in any of the lateral leads, including leads I, aVL, V5, and V6?

Does this ECG show a broad monophasic R wave in any of leads V5 and V6? Note that a broad monophasic R wave is defined as a positive R wave with a duration greater than 120 milliseconds and a R wave peak time greater than 55 milliseconds.
Which of the following leads show the broad monophasic R wave? Select all possible leads from the options below.

Within the selected leads, in which of the following segments can you observe the QRS complex with broad monophasic R wave? The options below refer to time ranges on the ECG strip, provided in seconds.
